Non-invasive Wearable Blood Glucose Monitor

Observe Glucose Curve After Eating (or Drinking Sugary Beverages)

After warm-up, you may eat or drink sugary beverages to observe
glucose changes.

Glucose response has a delay (approximately 15–30 minutes)
depending on the individual and the type of food.

Key Point: Long-term monitoring reveals how diet affects glucose
levels, helping you understand metabolic status and whether
glucose fluctuations occur.

Non-invasive Wearable Blood Glucose Monitor

Accuracy Range (MARD ~15%)

• Normal glucose range for healthy individuals: 100–150 mg/dl

• MARD (15%) — Mean Absolute Relative Difference

• Considering MARD ~15%, the display range for a normal

person (including potential error) will fall between 85–172.5
mg/dl

Tiilab CONFIDENTIAL

Non-invasive Wearable Blood Glucose Monitor

Accuracy Improvement Strategy

Hardware: HUSD & MBPF

Software: Algorithm Enhancement

System: Noise Suppression

• Using multi-band-pass filters (MBPF) to suppress unnecessary wavelengths and increase HUSD

brightness to improve sensing accuracy.

• Continue algorithm research to improve glucose trend feature extraction.
• Device companies must improve noise suppression for both optical and thermal noise, which is

essential for handling environmental variability.

Tiilab CONFIDENTIAL

Non-invasive Wearable Blood Glucose Monitor

Core Points

•

Individuals with normal glucose values 100–150 mg/dl are the primary test subjects.

• NICGM focuses on continuous long-term monitoring, which provides real-time wellness feedback.
• Single-point measurements only reflect the current glucose level.
• Continuous and long-term data reveals more physiological insights from glucose trends.
• Current objective: allow users to see real-time glucose changes to increase interest in NICGM usage.
• Pediatric growth clinics are potential users—excess sugar intake in children may cause attention issues,

hyperactivity, or affect development.

• With an aging population, glucose monitoring becomes increasingly important to reduce risks of chronic

diseases and improve quality of life.
